_comment: Functions the anon role is ALLOWED to execute in production, across every
scripts/check_anon_rpc_contract.py diffs live prod against this list on every
push touching supabase/, scripts/ or workflows, AND nightly on a schedule
(prod SQL is applied out of band here, so push-only triggers can miss a change).
Entries are FULL SIGNATURES, not bare names, on purpose. Keying on the name
alone let a new overload of an allowed name -- create function
public.beer_detail(text, text) -- collapse onto the existing entry and pass
CI green while being anon-callable via the default PUBLIC grant.
If CI fails on this file, decide before editing it:
- Meant to be public (guest browsing, public web)? Add it here WITH a
reason, in the same commit that grants it.
- Not meant to be public? Almost certainly the 0081 gotcha: CREATE FUNCTION
grants EXECUTE to PUBLIC, so anon inherits it. Fix with
revoke all on function public.<name>(<args>) from public;
then re-grant explicitly to the roles that should have it.
allowed: graphql_public.graphql("operationName" text, query text, variables jsonb, extensions jsonb)
public.brewery_map_feed_near(p_lat numeric, p_lng numeric, p_km integer, p_limit integer)
public.catalog_search(p_query text, p_style text, p_na_only boolean, p_limit integer, p_offset integer)
public.log_featured_event(p_featured uuid, p_event text, p_region text)

_notes.graphql_public.graphql: Supabase-managed GraphQL endpoint, owned by supabase_admin. Anon-callable by Supabase default and not ours to revoke. Listed so it is a conscious entry rather than an invisible one; it was outside the guard entirely until 2026-07-20.
_notes.log_featured_event: The only anon-callable function that WRITES. Intentional: it records impressions/clicks for partner placements from the public web. Its own body must keep validating input.
_notes.tapt_scan_name / tapt_trusted_country: Pure formatters used inside security_invoker views; anon needs execute for those views to resolve.
